sourceMovie.title: Death Race
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Death Race for the same reason it broke through in 2008: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Paul W. S. Anderson keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Terminator 2: Judgment Day
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Death Race clicked for you, Terminator 2: Judgment Day (1991) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Death Race?
faq.answer: Try Terminator 2: Judgment Day, Looper, The Running Man—each shares DNA with Death Race while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Death Race?
faq.answer: Death Race (2008) is best described as Action, Thriller, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Death Race part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Death Race (2008) Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
